info.name: StockBeat API Documentation
info.description: Token-authenticated REST API consumed by the StockBeat mobile app (React Native).

request.description: Queues a GDPR data export; the user receives it by email.

response.name: success

request.description: Schedules a GDPR soft-delete; the account is retained for a grace period before purge.

response.name: success

item.name: Analytics
item.description:
Analytics-lite (Plan §4.6): today/7d/30d revenue + order count + AOV, gated by plan
(`analytics_level`: Free=today only, Starter=+7d, Pro/Premium=full with comparison + goal
tracking). "Today" is always computed live, never from the `daily_stats` pre-aggregation.

request.description: `comparison` and `goal` are only present at the `full` analytics level (Pro/Premium).

response.name: range not allowed on this plan

request.description: Grouped by SKU, falling back to title for SKU-less line items. Same plan gating as the summary.

In-app banners currently active (within `starts_at`/`ends_at`) and matching the caller's
audience rules, newest first.

response.name: success

item.name: Auth — OTP
item.description: Passwordless sign-in: request a one-time code by email, then verify it for a bearer token.

request.description:
Always returns 200 regardless of whether the email is registered, to avoid account enumeration.
Rate limited (`otp-request`); a fresh code can be requested every 30s.

response.name: success

request.description:
Returns a Sanctum bearer token on success. `is_new_user` tells the client whether to route
into `/profile/setup` next. Rate limited (`otp-verify`); locks out after 5 failed attempts.

response.name: success
invalid or expired code

request.description:
One-time step for new users after OTP verification. Creates the user's owning `Team`
(with the user as `owner`) alongside setting their profile fields.
Revokes the bearer token used for this request only.
Revokes every Sanctum token for the user, not just the current one.

response.name: success

item.name: Connections
item.description:
Connect and manage storefront platforms (Shopify, WooCommerce, eBay, Etsy, Amazon).
Only WooCommerce is fully live end-to-end today — the others accept a connection
record but adapter operations are not yet available.

request.description:
`credentials` shape depends on `platform` — WooCommerce expects `{store_url, consumer_key,
consumer_secret}` (key-intake); OAuth-based platforms (Shopify/eBay/Etsy/Amazon) are
pending adapter approval and will return an OAuth URL once available.

response.name: profile setup incomplete

request.description:
Plain-language status for the connection-health screen — never raw error codes.
`fix_action` is a key the client maps to a concrete flow (e.g. `reauth`), not a URL.

response.name: healthy
needs reauth

request.description: Upserts the device's push token so the backend can deliver notifications to it.

Combines profile, current team/role, plan entitlements, and SMS credit balance —
the client's single call after launch/login. `needs_profile_setup` is true until
`/profile/setup` has been completed.

response.name: profile setup complete
profile setup pending

request.description: Most recent 50, newest first.

item.name: Orders
item.description:
The unified order feed across all connected channels, plus quick actions
(fulfill/tracking, refund, cancel, notes/tags, packing slip).

request.description:
Cursor-paginated, filterable by channel/store/status/date/value/tag, with global search
across order number, customer name/email, and item SKU/title. `history_days` is plan-gated
server-side; test orders are excluded unless the team is viewing in test mode.

response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": null,
  "data": {
    "orders": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "platform": "woo",
        "connection_id": 1,
        "order_number": "#1042",
        "status": "unfulfilled",
        "fulfillment_status": "unfulfilled",
        "payment_status": "paid",
        "currency": "AUD",
        "total": "84.00",
        "total_base_currency": "84.00",
        "customer_name": "Alex Chen",
        "customer_email": "alex@example.com",
        "shipping_address": { "line1": "1 Example St", "city": "Sydney", "postcode": "2000", "country": "AU" },
        "placed_at": "2026-07-16T00:30:00.000000Z",
        "ship_by_at": "2026-07-18T00:30:00.000000Z",
        "ship_by_hours_remaining": 46.5,
        "is_ship_by_urgent": false,
        "tags": ["gift"],
        "is_test": false,
        "snoozed_until": null
      }
    ],
    "next_cursor": "eyJpZCI6MSwiX3BvaW50c1RvTmV4dEl0ZW1zIjp0cnVlfQ"
  }
}
response.name: success

request.description:
Includes items and notes. 404s if the order isn't in the caller's team, or outside the
caller's `store_visibility` restriction.

response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": null,
  "data": {
    "order": {
      "id": 1,
      "platform": "woo",
      "connection_id": 1,
      "order_number": "#1042",
      "status": "unfulfilled",
      "fulfillment_status": "unfulfilled",
      "payment_status": "paid",
      "currency": "AUD",
      "total": "84.00",
      "total_base_currency": "84.00",
      "customer_name": "Alex Chen",
      "customer_email": "alex@example.com",
      "shipping_address": { "line1": "1 Example St", "city": "Sydney", "postcode": "2000", "country": "AU" },
      "placed_at": "2026-07-16T00:30:00.000000Z",
      "ship_by_at": "2026-07-18T00:30:00.000000Z",
      "ship_by_hours_remaining": 46.5,
      "is_ship_by_urgent": false,
      "tags": ["gift"],
      "is_test": false,
      "snoozed_until": null,
      "items": [
        { "id": 1, "sku": "VNT-014", "title": "Vintage Denim Jacket", "image_url": "https://example.com/img/vnt-014.jpg", "qty": 1, "price": "84.00" }
      ],
      "notes": []
    }
  }
}
response.name: success

item.name: Add a note to an order.

response.name: success

request.description: Pass `until` as an ISO-8601 datetime to snooze, or omit/null it to clear the snooze.

item.name: Fulfill an order with tracking info.

request.description:
Calls through to the real channel adapter where available (WooCommerce today); capability-checked
server-side, so this 422s with a plain-language message on platforms/actions that aren't supported yet.

response.name: capability not supported

request.description:
`amount` defaults to a full refund when omitted. Calls through to the real channel adapter
where available (WooCommerce today).
Calls through to the real channel adapter where available (WooCommerce today).
Returns the rendered PDF directly (`Content-Type: application/pdf`), not a JSON envelope.

item.name: Rules
item.description:
Custom notification rules: trigger + condition tree + actions, with quiet hours, cooldown,
and an execution log. See Plan §4.4 for the full trigger catalogue (12 triggers, e.g.
`new_order`, `unfulfilled_after_x`, `order_spike`, `low_stock`, `digest`).

response.name: success
success
profile setup incomplete
success

request.description:
Evaluates the rule immediately against real state (not a dry run) and logs the execution —
lets a merchant confirm a rule works before waiting for it to trigger naturally.
Most recent 50 firings, newest first.
Returns defaults (all channels enabled, no quiet hours) if the user has never saved any.

item.name: Support
item.description:
Live support chat (Plan §4.9). One thread per user, resumed across visits —
never a fresh thread. Internal staff notes (`direction=note`) are never
returned here.

response.name: success

request.description: Reopens the thread if it was previously resolved.

item.name: Team
item.description: Team seats and roles (owner/manager/staff), with per-member `store_visibility` restriction.

request.description: `store_visibility` optionally restricts the invited member to specific connection IDs.
